entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: Essential plant nutrient that is a major factor limiting crop productivity

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output, limited by nutrient availability

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Agricultural practices that meet current needs without compromising future generations

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia by certain organisms

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: Plant family capable of establishing root nodule symbiosis with rhizobia

entities.name: ROOT NODULE SYMBIOSIS
entities.description: Mutualistic association between legumes and nitrogen-fixing soil bacteria

entities.name: RHIZOBIA
entities.description: Collectively named nitrogen-fixing soil bacteria that interact with legumes

entities.name: BRADYRHIZOBIUM
entities.description: A specific genus of rhizobia that forms symbiotic relationships with groundnut

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., a legume characterized by the crack-entry bacterial invasion strategy

entities.name: CRACK-ENTRY MECHANISM
entities.description: A specific bacterial invasion strategy used by approximately 25% of legumes, including groundnut

entities.name: MOLECULAR SIGNALLING
entities.description: Complex communication between plants and rhizobia during the nodulation process

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Non-living environmental factors that affect root nodule symbiosis

entities.name: PHYTOHORMONE HOMEOSTASIS
entities.description: The balance of plant hormones related to the regulation of root nodule symbiosis

entities.name: GENE EXPRESSION
entities.description: The process by which genetic information is utilized, adjusted in response to symbiotic regulations
